title: Mining and proof of work
summary: Why PoW, ASICs and centralization, pool dynamics, difficulty adjustment, the security budget question, energy use in context.

edges.label: Why proof of work
edges.summary: What PoW actually buys that proof of stake doesn't: an external, unforgeable cost to writing history, and a Sybil resistance that doesn't depend on knowing who anyone is.

edges.label: The difficulty adjustment
edges.summary: Every 2,016 blocks the network retargets so blocks land roughly every ten minutes regardless of how much hashrate is online. The mechanism, the edge cases, and why it matters.

edges.label: ASICs and the mining-hardware industry
edges.summary: Why specialized hardware was inevitable, what it concentrates and what it doesn't, and how the global manufacturer market has actually shaken out.

edges.label: Pool dynamics and Stratum V2
edges.summary: Why miners join pools, how block-template construction has historically lived with the pool operator, and how Stratum V2 hands that decision back to individual miners.

edges.label: The security budget question
edges.summary: What pays for hashrate after the subsidy decays — fees, transaction demand, and the ongoing argument over whether that's enough.

edges.label: Energy use in context
edges.summary: Where the energy actually comes from in practice, why miners chase stranded and curtailed power, and how to read aggregate consumption numbers honestly.
